name: Spyre Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Spyre Therapeutics, Inc. is a clinical-stage biotechnology company pioneering long-acting antibodies and antibody combinations to redefine the standard of care for inflammatory bowel disease and rheumatic diseases. Spyre's pipeline includes extended half-life antibodies targeting α4β7, TL1A, and IL-23 in development as monotherapies and pair-wise combinations. We are advancing a pipeline of monoclonal antibodies for the treatment of IBD and other immune-mediated diseases.
sellsTo: patients with inflammatory bowel disease (ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease)
patients with rheumatic diseases (rheumatoid arthritis, psoriatic arthritis, axial spondyloarthritis)

exposures.sentence: We are advancing a portfolio of next-generation monotherapies - novel antibody candidates engineered for optimized potency, selectivity, and pharmacokinetics against validated IBD targets.

Our next-generation monotherapy antibody candidates targeting α4β7, TL1A, and IL-23 are engineered to match or exceed the potency of comparator first generation molecules, maintain selectivity, incorporate Fc domain modifications called YTE substitutions in order to increase pharmacokinetic half-life, and are formulated as high-concentration, citrate-free formulations.

Our goal is to develop next-generation therapeutics to redefine the standard of care for the treatment of IBD and other immune-mediated diseases, relying on three strategic pillars: Advancing a portfolio of next-generation monotherapies ... Evaluating paradigm-changing IBD combinations ... Expansion of our anti-TL1A program into additional indications

catalysts.event: SKYLINE Phase 2 platform trial Part A induction data readout
catalysts.sentence: Part A: Open-label assessment of the safety and preliminary efficacy of a single dose level of each investigational monotherapy, with induction data expected to begin reading out in Q2 2026.

catalysts.event: SKYWAY Phase 2 basket trial topline proof-of-concept data
catalysts.sentence: Topline proof-of-concept data for all three indications are expected in Q4 2026.

tamClaims.claim: 2.4 million individuals in the US have IBD
tamClaims.sentence: In the United States, it is estimated that approximately 2.4 million individuals currently have IBD, with approximately 70,000 patients newly diagnosed every year.

tamClaims.claim: 1.5 million individuals in the US have RA
tamClaims.sentence: RA affects more than 1.5 million individuals in the U.S., with stable-to-increasing incidence of ~40 per 100,000 individuals.

tamClaims.claim: 0.8 million individuals in the US have PsA
tamClaims.sentence: PsA affects approximately 0.8 million individuals in the U.S., with estimates suggesting that up to 15% of PsO patients may have undiagnosed PsA.

tamClaims.claim: 3 million individuals in the US have axSpA, with 1 million diagnosed
tamClaims.sentence: AxSpA affects an estimated 3 million individuals in the U.S., though only approximately 1 million of those are diagnosed.
